Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query "Marcus Webb" in both INBOX and SENT folders to trace the email mix-up chain. The agent discovers that Blessing sent Canopy Marcus Webb's Chicago apartment options to marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com (BrightLoop's Marcus Webb, Atlanta to Boston) instead of marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com (Canopy's Marcus Webb, Detroit to Chicago). BrightLoop's Tessa Moreno then forwarded the email to Canopy Marcus thinking it related to his cryogenic equipment shipment, creating the confusion.
OE 2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Marcus Webb" to find internal discussions about the mix-up and the split-vendor arrangement. The agent discovers Slack threads covering the Heartland and Swift split-vendor coordination, Suki Patel flagging the duplicate name as a data hygiene issue, and details about the cold-chain requirements for Marcus Webb's cryogenic lab equipment.
OE 3: Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts with query "Marcus Webb" to find the BrightLoop Marcus Webb contact record (marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com). Additionally, search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Marcus Webb" to find the Canopy Health Marcus Webb contact record (marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com, phone 313-555-0294). Note: the two Marcus Webbs live in different services — BrightLoop's record is in the Contacts service while Canopy's record is in the CRM — and the agent must disambiguate between them.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with query such as "UrbanNest" or "apartment options Chicago" to find the original UrbanNest apartment listings sourced for Canopy Marcus's Chicago relocation. The agent discovers three options: River North at 500 N State St Unit 2204 for $2,400 per month, West Loop at 170 N Halsted St Unit 1508 for $2,200 per month, and South Loop at 1255 S State St Unit 910 for $2,050 per month.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with query such as "Swift cold-chain freight" or "Swift Relocations" to determine whether the cold-chain freight quote for Marcus Webb's cryogenic lab equipment ever came back. The agent discovers the quote was requested with a deadline of April 5 and has not been received, making it overdue past the April 5 deadline. The quote covers temperature-controlled transport of two Thermo CryoPlus 3800 containers and one MVE SC 20/20 Dewar flask from Detroit to Chicago, requiring sustained negative 20 degrees Celsius.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with query such as "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" to check on the BrightLoop Marcus Webb's car shipment delay. The agent discovers that Road Runner Auto Transport flagged a delay on April 11 for Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic, stuck at the Indianapolis transfer hub with a revised delivery window of April 18 to 20. BrightLoop Marcus sent two follow-up emails that went unanswered. Julian Brooks later handled recovery communications.
OE 7: Look up contacts for Greg Pallone using contacts_search_contacts with query "Greg" or "Greg Pallone". The agent discovers Greg Pallone's email is greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, which is needed for the Swift freight quote follow-up email.
OE 8: Look up contacts for Catalina Dubois using contacts_search_contacts with query "Catalina". The agent discovers Catalina's email is catalina.dubois@moveops.com, which is needed for the heads-up email.
OE 9: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value "Marcus Webb" to find all Marcus Webb records. The agent discovers three records: recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago (Canopy Health, Detroit to Chicago, In Progress, Blessing Okafor coordinating), recMarcusWebbBrightloop (BrightLoop Analytics, Atlanta to Boston, In Progress, Suki Patel coordinating), and rec_reloc_webb (BrightLoop Analytics Q1 completed, Atlanta to Denver).
OE 10: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value "Canopy" to find all active Canopy Health moves. The agent discovers: Dr. Renata Kovacs (Nashville to Toronto, April 14 to 17, Fatimah Al-Rashidi coordinating, international cross-border with customs complexity), Tyler Okonkwo (Philadelphia to Denver, April 12 to 14, Javier Morales coordinating, 13-day storage gap until May 1 lease start), Marcus Webb (Detroit to Chicago), Dr. Yusuf Abdi (Completed), and Lily Marchetti (status may show Completed but was actually Cancelled).
OE 11: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria referencing Canopy or the invoice number INV-2026-0401 to check Canopy invoicing status. The agent discovers invoice INV-2026-0401 totaling $9,210, due May 4, with $2,650 in surcharges still pending Patricia Langford's written approval: $850 customs broker for Kovacs, $1,200 cold-chain freight for Webb, and $600 temp storage for Okonkwo.
OE 12: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "Canopy" to check for open Canopy-related tickets. The agent discovers Linear issues related to the Canopy Q2 relocations and may find that Lily Marchetti tickets remain open despite her relocation being cancelled in February.
OE 13: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with filter_users_from set to Chloe Vance's user ID (moveops_chloe_vance) and filter_in_im_or_mpim to find Chloe's direct message to Blessing about hearing about moves from clients instead of from Blessing. The agent attempts to locate the specific DM content. Chloe's broader pattern of concern about Blessing's communication is documented across multiple public messages in the operations channel referencing the Road Runner delay miss, the Mosaic prototype packing sign-off gap, and coordinator overload.
OE 14: Reply to Marcus Webb (Canopy Health) using reply_to_email with sender blessing.okafor@moveops.com. The reply must include his three correct Chicago apartment options from UrbanNest (River North at $2,400, West Loop at $2,200, South Loop at $2,050), an honest status that the Swift cold-chain freight quote has been overdue since April 5 and is being chased, and an apology for the email mix-up. The agent should also note that the Canopy Health Chicago lab receiving dock address needed by Swift is still TBD — per the Airtable record and CRM engagement, this address was to be obtained from Patricia Langford via Catalina Dubois.
OE 15: Send email to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations using send_email with sender blessing.okafor@moveops.com and recipients greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com. The email should urgently request the overdue cold-chain freight quote for Marcus Webb's three cryogenic lab items (two Thermo CryoPlus 3800 containers at roughly 65 lbs each and one MVE SC 20/20 Dewar flask at roughly 30 lbs), route Detroit MI to Chicago IL, requiring temperature-controlled transport at negative 20 degrees Celsius or below.
OE 16: Send email to Catalina Dubois using send_email with sender blessing.okafor@moveops.com and recipients catalina.dubois@moveops.com. The email should give Catalina a heads-up before Patricia Langford calls, covering: the Marcus Webb apartment mix-up and resolution, status of all active Canopy Q2 moves (Marcus Webb, Dr. Kovacs, Tyler Okonkwo), the Dr. Anand Mehta executive relocation, invoice INV-2026-0401 with $2,650 in unapproved surcharges, and key risks Patricia may raise.
OE 17: Post a message in the operations channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id C006 and payload covering the Marcus Webb email mix-up, the overdue Swift freight quote, status on the BrightLoop Marcus Webb car delay, Canopy Q2 wave status for awareness, and the process fix for duplicate names. The message should address Chloe Vance's concern about hearing about moves from clients rather than from Blessing directly.
OE 18: Update both active Marcus Webb Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001 and table_id tblRelocations01. Update record recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago to change the Name field to "Marcus Webb (Canopy Health)" and add a duplicate name warning with correct email address (marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com) in the Special Requirements field. Update record recMarcusWebbBrightloop to change the Name field to "Marcus Webb (BrightLoop Analytics)" and add a duplicate name warning with correct email address (marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com) in the Special Requirements field.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use reply_to_email or send_email with sender [blessing.okafor@moveops.com](mailto:blessing.okafor@moveops.com) and recipient (or recipients including) [marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com](mailto:marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com) to email Marcus Webb at Canopy Health.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used reply_to_email with sender 'blessing.okafor@moveops.com' and email_id 'email_marcus_webb_canopy_confused', which is the email from Marcus Webb at Canopy Health. reply_to_email implicitly sends to the original sender of that email.
✓Run #2The agent used reply_to_email with sender 'blessing.okafor@moveops.com' and email_id 'email_marcus_webb_canopy_confused', which is the email from Canopy's Marcus Webb (marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com). The reply goes to the sender of that email, who is Marcus Webb at Canopy Health.
✓Run #3Item 84 shows a send_email call with sender blessing.okafor@moveops.com and recipients [marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com]. The call was successful (email_id returned).
✓Run #4The agent used reply_to_email with sender blessing.okafor@moveops.com replying to email_id 'email_marcus_webb_canopy_confused', which is the email from Canopy Marcus Webb (marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com). The reply goes to that address by default.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'blessing.okafor@moveops.com' and recipients ['marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com'], which satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'blessing.okafor@moveops.com' and recipients ['marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com']. PASS.
2 The email sent to [marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com](mailto:marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com) must include three Chicago apartment options from UrbanNest: River North at approximately $2,400 per month, West Loop at approximately $2,200 per month, and South Loop at approximately $2,050 per month (or similar descriptions of these three options)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The email content explicitly lists all three apartment options: River North at 500 N State St for $2,400/month, West Loop at 170 N Halsted St for $2,200/month, and South Loop at 1255 S State St for $2,050/month.
✓Run #2The reply email content explicitly lists all three options: River North at 500 N State St for $2,400/month, West Loop at 170 N Halsted St for $2,200/month, and South Loop at 1255 S State St for $2,050/month.
✓Run #3The email content in item 84 includes all three UrbanNest options: River North at 500 N State St for $2,400/month, West Loop at 170 N Halsted St for $2,200/month, and South Loop at 1255 S State St for $2,050/month.
✗Run #4The reply email to Canopy Marcus Webb only 'promised apartment options to be resent to correct email' but did not actually include the three UrbanNest Chicago apartment options (River North ~$2,400, West Loop ~$2,200, South Loop ~$2,050) in the email body.
✓Run #5The email to marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com explicitly includes all three apartment options: River North at $2,400/month (500 N State St), West Loop at $2,200/month (170 N Halsted St), and South Loop at $2,050/month (1255 S State St).
✗Run #6The email sent to marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com states 'I am working with our housing partner right now to finalize your correct Chicago apartment options and will have them to you within 24 hours.' It does NOT include any of the three UrbanNest Chicago apartment options (River North ~$2,400, West Loop ~$2,200, South Loop ~$2,050). The agent committed to sending them later rather than including them in the email. FAIL.
3 The email sent to [marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com](mailto:marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com) must mention that the Swift cold-chain freight quote has not been received and is overdue (originally due around April 5, which is 21 days before the current date of April 26, 2026).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to marcus.webb.lab@gmail.com explicitly states: 'this quote is overdue. It was supposed to be back by April 5 and I have not yet received it.' This clearly conveys the quote is overdue.
✓Run #2The reply to Marcus Webb states 'The quote has taken longer than it should have' and the email to Greg at Swift explicitly says it 'was originally due back on April 5 and is now over three weeks overdue.' The email to Marcus is honest about the delay.
✓Run #3The email in item 84 explicitly states the Swift freight quote 'was originally due back on April 5 and is overdue,' clearly communicating the 3-week delay to Marcus Webb.
✓Run #4The reply email content explicitly acknowledged the Swift quote is overdue and being chased. The agent also notes the quote was due April 5, consistent with it being 21 days overdue.
